text.title: GraphQL API Design Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns observed in real deployments. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: New Query/Mutation field ships without permission_classes; data silently exposed.
antipattern.root-cause: Author forgot to declare permission; no enforced gate.
antipattern.fix: Add a CI check that requires permission_classes on every Mutation/Query field touching auth-scoped data.

antipattern.symptom: users(offset:0, limit:20) — duplicates and gaps under concurrent writes.
antipattern.root-cause: Author copied REST pagination 1:1 into GraphQL.
antipattern.fix: Convert to Relay cursor pagination (first/after with Connection/Edge/PageInfo).

antipattern.symptom: DataLoader instantiated at module scope; cross-request cache leaks user data.
antipattern.root-cause: Misreading DataLoader README — it is a per-request batching cache, not a global one.
antipattern.fix: Move loader instantiation into get_context(); pin a unit test asserting loaders are created per request.

antipattern.symptom: Nested list resolver triggers one DB query per parent; passes unit tests but quadratic in prod.
antipattern.root-cause: Resolver author did not add DataLoader.
antipattern.fix: Wrap the relation in a DataLoader; assert query count < N+constant in an integration test.
